concerns.text: FR-009 validates the agency score against a 'therapeutic alliance scale' (target r ≥ 0.30). However, therapeutic alliance and perceived agency are distinct psychological constructs. Validating agency against alliance tests convergent validity for the wrong construct. The spec should validate against an established 'perceived agency' or 'autonomy' scale, or test discriminant validity to show the measure is specific to agency.

FR-005 specifies running 4 separate regression tests (one per adherence metric) without addressing multiple testing correction. This inflates Type I error rate. The spec should specify a correction method (e.g., Bonferroni, FDR) or pre-register a primary outcome to control family-wise error.

FR-004 and FR-011 include 'self-reported engagement' as an adherence metric. If collected via survey during the same period as conversations, this introduces common-method bias with linguistic markers (both from same time period, potentially influenced by same factors). The spec should address this by using only objective metrics or controlling for common-method variance.

FR-010 allows confounders to be 'omitted with a logged note' if unavailable. Systematic missingness in confounders (e.g., baseline severity) could introduce selection bias. The spec should specify how missing confounders will be handled (e.g., multiple imputation, sensitivity analysis, or complete-case analysis with bias assessment).
